title: convolvulus supinus var. supinus
intro: Convolvulus supinus var. supinus, also known as the creeping bindweed or dwarf morning glory, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as convaltol and pseudopelletierine. The health benefits of Convolvulus supinus var. supinus include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. Convolvulus supinus var. supinus can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal preparation methods.
